---
title: "Dr Thomas Goodge"
---

**Research Associate**  
*Glasgow Interactive Systems Group (GIST), School of Computing Science, University of Glasgow*

[Email](mailto:thomasgoodgeresearch@gmail.com) | 
[GitHub](https://github.com/thomasgoodge/) | 
[Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=mzytrugAAAAJ&hl=en) | 
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/thomas-goodge-9097a6a1/)

---

## Profile

I am a **Research Associate** in the **Glasgow Interactive Systems Group (GIST)**. My research explores the interaction between **Cognitive Psychology** and **Human–Computer Interaction**, focusing on:

- Visual attention  
- Decision-making  
- Mixed reality and AI systems  

This includes work on **visual salience**, **LLMs/VLMs**, and **risk appraisal in AI-mediated environments**.

---

## Qualifications

### 2020–2025 — PhD, University of Glasgow (Social AI CDT)
- Focus: Augmented Reality interfaces for driver awareness  
- Methods: Cognitive processing + situational awareness  
- *Shortlisted: SICSA Best PhD Dissertation 2025*

**Thesis**  
*Investigating the Effects of Augmented Reality Cues During Non-Driving Related Tasks on the Situational Awareness of Drivers*

---

### 2016–2020 — MSc Information Security (Merit)  
Royal Holloway, University of London (Distance Learning)

- Focus: Cognitive processing and risk online  
- Modules:
  - Human Aspects of Information Security  
  - Cybercrime  
  - Security Management  
  - Computer Security  
  - Cryptography  

---

### 2014–2015 — MSc Psychological Research Methods (Merit)  
University of Nottingham  

- Focus: Research design and evaluation across psychological domains  
- Modules:
  - Empirical Research Project  
  - Advanced Research Methods  
  - Internship  

---

### 2014 — TEFL (140-hour Advanced Course)

---

### 2011–2014 — BSc (Hons) Psychology (2:1)  
University of Nottingham  

- Key modules:
  - Cognitive Psychology in the Real World  
  - Psychology of Digital Technology  
  - Vision  

---

## Research Skills

**Quantitative Methods**
